model.label: Claude Sonnet 4.6
model.description: Anthropic • Fast flagship Claude model for coding, analysis, and high-throughput agent use.

pick.rationale: Purdue enters this Round of 32 matchup as one of the nation's elite programs — KenPom #8 and Torvik #8 with an AdjEM of +30.5 and the nation's top-ranked adjusted offense (AdjOE 132.6 by Torvik) — representing a roughly 10-point efficiency gap over Miami (FL)'s KenPom #30 (AdjEM +20.67) profile. The Boilermakers arrive in peak form as Big Ten Tournament champions, having dismantled #1 Michigan 80-72 in the final with Braden Smith (11 assists, Tournament MVP), Oscar Cluff (21 pts), and Trey Kaufman-Renn (20 pts) all healthy and executing at an elite level. Miami's defense ranked 45th nationally (AdjDE 101.5) is a meaningful liability against Purdue's historically prolific offense, and while the Hurricanes' ACC battle-testing and solid two-way efficiency profile give them a credible upset path — 7-over-2 upsets occur at roughly a 25-30% historical rate — the combination of Purdue's superior efficiency margin, peak health, and dominant recent form make the Boilermakers a clear and confident pick to advance to the Sweet 16.

reasoningStep.title: Purdue vs Miami (FL) — Round of 32 West Region Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Purdue holds a commanding ~10-point adjusted efficiency margin advantage over Miami (FL), arrives as Big Ten Tournament champions in peak health, and possesses the nation's most prolific offense that Miami's 45th-ranked defense is ill-equipped to contain. Miami's ACC credentials and solid two-way profile give them a credible but ultimately insufficient upset path against a top-10 national program.
evidence: Purdue: KenPom #8 (AdjEM +30.5, AdjO 131 [#2], AdjD 100.5); Torvik #8 (AdjOE 132.6 [#1 nationally], AdjDE 100.2 [#36]). Miami (FL): KenPom #30 (AdjEM +20.67, AdjO 121.4 [#34], AdjD 100.7); Torvik #35 (AdjOE 121.1 [#35], AdjDE 101.5 [#45]). Efficiency gap: ~10 points in AdjEM.

Purdue won the Big Ten Tournament championship over #1 Michigan 80-72. Braden Smith (11 assists, Big Ten Tournament MVP), Oscar Cluff (21 pts), Trey Kaufman-Renn (20 pts) all healthy and contributing at elite levels heading into the NCAA Tournament.

Miami (FL) is a 7-seed under first-year coach Jai Lucas with a 25-8 ACC record. Advanced past Missouri in Round of 64. No significant injury concerns surfaced, but first-year coaching staff introduces some uncertainty in tournament preparation.

2-seeds defeat 7-seeds at approximately 70-75% historically. Purdue's ~10-point efficiency gap and nation's #1 adjusted offense make this one of the stronger 2-seed profiles in the bracket, reducing Miami's upset probability below the historical baseline.
